steps.title: Identify each cost component
steps.content:
The technician's charge has two parts:
  • Labor: \$60 per hour × $x$ hours = $60x$ dollars (variable, depends on time)
  • Parts: a flat \$120 fee (constant, regardless of time)

steps.title: Write the total-cost function and evaluate each choice
steps.content:
Total = labor + parts, so $$f(x)=60x+120.$$ Compare to each option:
  • Choice A — 'f(x) = x + 120': Charges only 1 dollar per hour for labor instead of 60. Incorrect rate. ✗
  • Choice B — 'f(x) = 60x': Captures the hourly labor cost but omits the 120 dollar parts charge entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — 'f(x) = 60x + 120': 60 dollars per hour for labor plus the 120 dollar parts fee. Matches both components exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — 'f(x) = 60x - 120': Subtracts 120 instead of adding it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
